content:
<system_instruction>
You are working inside Conductor, a Mac app that lets the user run many coding agents in parallel.
Your work should take place in the /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/manila directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.
The target branch for this workspace is main. Use this for actions like creating new PRs, bisecting, etc., unless you're told otherwise.

If the user asks you to work on several unrelated tasks in parallel, you can suggest they start new workspaces.
If the user asks for help with Conductor, ask them to email [humans@conductor.build](mailto:humans@conductor.build) or
click on the comment icon in the bottom left to send feedback.
</system_instruction>

<system_instruction>
You are working inside Conductor, a Mac app that lets the user run many coding agents in parallel.
Your work should take place in the /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/manila directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.
The target branch for this workspace is main. Use this for actions like creating new PRs, bisecting, etc., unless you're told otherwise.

If the user asks you to work on several unrelated tasks in parallel, you can suggest they start new workspaces.
If the user asks for help with Conductor, ask them to email [humans@conductor.build](mailto:humans@conductor.build) or
click on the comment icon in the bottom left to send feedback.
</system_instruction>

content.thinking: Let me look at the failing smoke tests to understand what's going on.

The test file references `signInAndGetSession` at line 236 but it's defined at line 336. The setup test at line 178 calls `signInAndGetSession` which is fine. But the issue is that the setup test (line 178) needs to succeed for the other tests to have `fullToken` and `workspaceId`.

Let me look at the actual error messages. The user said these 4 tests are failing but didn't share the error output. Let me run the tests to see what's happening.

Wait, let me first check what the current state of the modified files is, since git status shows changes to chat-processor.ts, create-work-item.ts, and persist-extraction.ts.


I should just run the failing tests directly to see what errors are actually occurring.

content.text: Let me run the tests to see the actual errors.

content.text:
All 8 tests pass locally. The failures you saw might have been from a previous run before recent fixes were applied. Current state is green:

```
(pass) setup: create workspace and person with OAuth tokens [263ms]
(pass) MCP route with valid JWT returns data [17ms]
(pass) MCP route without token returns 401
(pass) MCP route with invalid token returns 401
(pass) MCP route with wrong workspace returns 403 or 404
(pass) scope enforcement: read-only token blocked from write routes [145ms]
(pass) token refresh returns new access token [137ms]
(pass) .well-known/oauth-protected-resource returns metadata
```

Was this from a CI run? If so, the issue might be environment-specific (missing seed data, JWKS timing, etc.). Share the CI logs and I can dig in.
